content: The language education definition of reflexion as a core metacognitive learning practice for human students can be borrowed to redesign LLM self-reflection mechanisms: instead of only engineering algorithmic reflection on output errors, we can frame LLM reflexion as a structured 'learning journal' practice that mirrors how high school students build long-term mastery through repeated deliberate reflection. This framework turns ad-hoc LLM self-reflection into a cumulative, growth-aligned process tied to incremental skill refinement just like human learning.
